patches.replace:
not uniquely diagnostic of the dome's specific toroidal architecture.

A fourth charitable reading drops the sub-terranean framing entirely: perhaps C = 64,852 nT describes a second surface aperture — the point above the Sump where the toroidal flow re-enters the Earth at the Antarctic barrier — making A and C the field magnitudes at two symmetric surface apertures rather than a surface value paired with a sub-terranean return density. The kernel is real: a closed two-aperture circuit demands similar field magnitudes at both ends, and the dome's fitted A and C are similar (~62,000–65,000 nT). But this rescue fails on two independent grounds. (i) Symmetric flux conservation requires A = C exactly (two apertures of equal area must produce equal surface fields: AN × BN = AS × BS with AN = AS forces BN = BS). The dome's fitted values are not equal — they differ by ~4% (62,376 nT vs 64,852 nT) — and that 4% asymmetry tracks the observed WMM2025 / IGRF-13 pole-strength asymmetry to within fitting noise. So either the coefficients are tuned to globe observations (a curve fit, not a derivation from any symmetric dome geometry) or the dome is implicitly borrowing the asymmetric-geodynamo physics it was built to leave behind. (ii) Both coefficients carry positive signs in B(r), so the dome treats both apertures as flux sources rather than a source-and-sink pair. In any closed magnetic circuit one aperture must be a sink — the directional sign contradiction documented above is unchanged by re-interpreting C as a second surface aperture. Re-interpreting the south coefficient relocates the contradictions; it does not eliminate them.

A second self-contradiction concerns the equation's functional form.
patches.rationale: EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1243: Insert surface-aperture re-interpretation rebuttal paragraph between narrow-channel rescue close and functional-form contradiction. Closes ISS-1243 moderate defender pivot hole.
Fix broken Finlay DOI 10.1038/nature21807 (does not exist per CrossRef) to correct CHAOS-6 DOI 10.1186/s40623-016-0486-1 (Finlay et al. 2016, Earth Planets Space — confirmed via CrossRef API).

patches.find: The +0.34 W/m² figure cited by the dome is a decadal trend
patches.replace: The +0.34 W/m²/decade figure cited by the dome is a decadal trend
patches.rationale: Standardize +0.34 W/m²/decade unit in WIN-066.detail_evidence to match part2 section usage. Minor unit inconsistency fix per ISS-2013.

patches.find: WIN-029 Schumann frequency (the dome's own H(r) produces a fundamental cavity resonance ~8.78 Hz (quarter-wave at H_max = 8,537 km), not the observed 7.83 Hz). Each dimension is independent and closes on the dome's own commitments.
patches.replace: WIN-038 Schumann frequency (the dome's own H(r) produces a fundamental cavity resonance ~8.78 Hz (quarter-wave at H_max = 8,537 km), not the observed 7.83 Hz). Each test is independent — one EM-opacity argument instantiated at two frequencies (radio astronomy + CMB observability) and one Schumann-cavity argument — and closes on the dome's own commitments.
patches.rationale: Fix cross-WIN attribution (WIN-029 Schumann is actually WIN-038 per curmudgeon c4 HOLE 0) and clarify independence claim (c4 HOLE 1 carry-over from c3): each test is independent, specifying one EM-opacity argument at two frequencies plus one Schumann-cavity argument.

patches.find: dome's WIN-029 Schumann frequency derives ~8.78 Hz (quarter-wave at H_max = 8,537 km) from the dome's own H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619), not the observed 7.83 Hz. Three independent self-contradictions on the dome's own commitments — pattern (2) SC.
